items.question: How do you know a notebook is reproducible?
items.answer: Run Restart & Run All from a fresh kernel: if every cell executes top-to-bottom without error and produces the expected results, it doesn't depend on hidden state.

items.question: Why do notebooks cause painful version-control diffs?
items.answer: They're stored as JSON with embedded outputs and execution counts, so even tiny changes produce large, noisy diffs. Stripping outputs (nbstripout) or pairing a .py (jupytext) fixes this.

items.question: What breaks when you copy-paste the same helper into many notebooks?
items.answer: The copies drift out of sync and can't be tested centrally; a fix in one notebook doesn't reach the others. Extracting to a shared module avoids this.
